summary: Legacy arbiter: non-deterministic grant ordering after reset during active transaction
description:
In the legacy round-robin arbiter (v1.2), if a reset is asserted WHILE an active transaction is in progress (i.e., a grant is already being held by a port), the post-reset grant ordering may not start from port 0. This is because the transaction-in-progress logic prevents the last_grant pointer from being fully cleared during reset.

This behavior was observed in silicon on the Alpha-7 chip and was determined to be acceptable because:
1. Reset during active transaction is an exceptional condition (not normal operation)
2. The system-level reset sequence always ensures no active transactions before asserting reset
3. The protocol spec at the time (v2.1) did not explicitly define post-reset behavior during active transactions

Resolution: The checker was updated to allow non-deterministic post-reset grant ordering ONLY when reset occurs during an active transaction. Normal clean-reset behavior (reset with no active transaction) still requires grant to start from port 0.
conditions_for_exception: Reset asserted DURING an active transaction (grant held by a port)
System-level reset sequence guarantees no functional impact

comments.author: mike.zhang
comments.text: Confirmed this only happens when grant is active during reset assertion. Normal reset path works correctly — last_grant properly clears to 0.

comments.author: sarah.park
comments.text: Added checker waiver for the specific condition: reset_during_active_txn. Clean reset path checker remains unchanged.

comments.author: david.liu
comments.text: Closing as Won't Fix. The exception is documented and the checker has been updated to handle this specific case.

related_spec_versions: v2.1 (did not define post-reset behavior during active txn)
